user.title: Centralite bridge connection
user.description: Select your system type and the serial port that connects to the Centralite bridge.

data_description.baud: Default is 19200; change only if you modified the bridge default.

import.title: Import names and device IDs
import.description: Paste your Elegance .elg export into the first field to bulk-import friendly names, scene names, and the list of devices to expose. Or enter comma-separated IDs in the other fields if you don't have an export file. (JetStream .jts import is not supported yet — for JetStream, enter IDs manually; friendly names come from the bridge's on-device name query.)

error.import_failed: Could not parse the provided text as an Elegance .elg file. JetStream .jts files are not supported yet — enter IDs manually in the comma-separated fields instead.
error.ids_out_of_range: One or more IDs are outside the valid range for the selected system. Check your load, scene, and switch IDs.

init.title: Centralite options
init.description: Tune how the integration polls the bridge for state changes.

data_description.poll_interval: Elegance only: how often to send a full ^G query to catch any state drift. Push events are the primary update path; this is a safety net for loads that aren't programmed for spontaneous output. JetStream is push-only and ignores this setting.

v1_migration.title: Centralite v1 entities were migrated
v1_migration.description:
Your previous Centralite integration's entities have been adopted into the new v2 integration:

- {migrated_count} entities were renamed to the v2 unique_id format (areas, aliases, icons, and dashboard placements were preserved).
- {deleted_count} obsolete entities were removed (scene `-OFF` entities are no longer separate; they are absorbed into a single scene switch).

**You may need to update automations or dashboards** that referenced the old entity IDs. Home Assistant does not automatically rewrite YAML automation files.

Migration log:

{summary}
